sources.title: Quadrax (+ Optional Qx Expander) Manual — Firmware 1.2.1

name: Qx
description: 4HP expander for Quadrax (also works with Gx). Adds one End-of-Rise and one End-of-Fall gate output per Quadrax channel — 8 gate outs total. Behavior follows each channel's current Quadrax mode (AD/AHR/CYCLE/BURST/LFO).

outputs.name: EOR 1
outputs.description: End-of-Rise gate for Quadrax CH 1. Fires when the rise stage completes. Exact behavior depends on CH 1's mode (see behaviors).

outputs.name: EOF 1
outputs.description: End-of-Fall gate for Quadrax CH 1. Fires when the fall/release stage completes. Exact behavior depends on CH 1's mode.

behaviors.name: AD mode gates
behaviors.description: EOR goes high at the peak of the attack and stays high for the entire decay, then drops to 0V. EOF is the inverse: high during rise, goes low at the attack peak, stays low through the decay, then goes high at the end of decay.

behaviors.name: AHR mode gates
behaviors.description: EOR goes high at the end of attack and stays high through hold and release. EOF goes low at the start of the release phase and high at the end of release.

behaviors.name: CYCLE mode gates
behaviors.description: EOR is high during the decay half of each cycle, low during rise. EOF is the inverse (180° opposite). Effectively a square wave at the LFO rate on each output — useful as a clock or complementary gate pair.

behaviors.name: BURST mode gates
behaviors.description: EOR pulses high at the start of every individual pulse inside the burst (per-pulse clock). EOF goes high once at the very end of the whole burst (burst-complete flag).

behaviors.name: LFO mode gates (STANDARD)
behaviors.description: EOR goes high at the LFO midpoint and drops when the cycle resets — a square at LFO rate. EOF is the inverse (180° opposite): high for the first half of the cycle, low for the second.

behaviors.name: LFO mode gates (ALTERNATE)
behaviors.trigger: Quadrax channel set to LFO (alternate / LFV)
behaviors.description: EOR is high whenever the LFO voltage is moving negative; EOF is high whenever it's moving positive. Complementary direction flags from the chaotic vacillator.

behaviors.name: Works with Gx
behaviors.trigger: Gx used in place of Qx
behaviors.description: Identical circuit with generic labels 1–8 instead of EOR/EOF pairs. Connects to Quadrax via I2C the same way.

patchIdeas.name: Cascaded 4-stage envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Use EOF gates to chain each Quadrax AD envelope into the next.
steps: Set Quadrax CH 1–4 all to AD mode (MODE button unlit).
Dial Rise and Fall on each channel to taste — CH 1 short, CH 2 medium, CH 3 long, CH 4 long release.
Patch your trigger source → Quadrax CH 1 TRIG.
Patch Qx EOF 1 → Quadrax CH 2 TRIG.
Patch Qx EOF 2 → Quadrax CH 3 TRIG.
Patch Qx EOF 3 → Quadrax CH 4 TRIG.
Patch Quadrax OUT 4 → VCA CV to hear the final stage (or mix OUT 1–4 for a running envelope).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Quadrax OUT 4 — the final envelope stage, 0–5V or 0–10V depending on System Mode.
patchIdeas.hear: One incoming trigger unrolls a four-part envelope: stage 1 fires, stage 2 waits for 1 to finish and then rises, and so on. Great for long evolving pads or multi-stage percussion hits.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QX + QUADRAX:
* CH 1–4: AD mode

- Trigger Source (Trig) t> Quadrax (CH 1 TRIG)
- Qx (EOF 1) t> Quadrax (CH 2 TRIG)
- Qx (EOF 2) t> Quadrax (CH 3 TRIG)
- Qx (EOF 3) t> Quadrax (CH 4 TRIG)
- Quadrax (OUT 4) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Per-pulse drum fills from BURST
patchIdeas.patch: BURST mode + EOR gives one trigger per pulse inside the burst — EOF flags the end.
steps: Set Quadrax CH 1 to BURST mode (MODE button yellow).
Set CH 1 Rise for pulse rate (noon ≈ musical 16th-note range).
Set CH 1 Fall for the overall burst length (how many pulses).
Patch your clock → Quadrax CH 1 TRIG to fire a burst per beat.
Patch Qx EOR 1 → drum-voice TRIG — one hit per pulse inside the burst.
Patch Qx EOF 1 → a second voice's TRIG (e.g. crash) — fires once when the burst ends.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Qx EOR 1 — 0–5V gate per pulse. Qx EOF 1 — 0–5V gate at burst end.
patchIdeas.hear: A rolling drum fill on EOR that ends with an accent on EOF. Change CH 1 Rise to speed up or slow down the roll; change Fall to make the fill longer or shorter.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QX + QUADRAX:
* CH 1: BURST mode

- Clock (Clk) c> Quadrax (CH 1 TRIG)
- Qx (EOR 1) t> Drum Voice (Trig) // per-pulse hits
- Qx (EOF 1) t> Crash (Trig) // end-of-burst accent

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature clocks from one LFO
patchIdeas.patch: A single CYCLE channel gives you two square clocks 180° apart on EOR and EOF.
steps: Set Quadrax CH 1 to CYCLE mode (MODE button green).
Set CH 1 Rise and Fall to match — this sets the LFO period.
Patch Qx EOR 1 → Sequencer A clock input.
Patch Qx EOF 1 → Sequencer B clock input.
Patch Quadrax OUT 1 → Filter Cutoff for the triangle LFO itself.
patchIdeas.signalOut: EOR 1 and EOF 1 — two 0–5V square clocks, phase-offset. OUT 1 — the triangle LFO itself.
patchIdeas.hear: Two sequencers step in alternation off the same LFO, while the filter breathes with the underlying triangle. Instant polyrhythm from one channel.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QX + QUADRAX:
* CH 1: CYCLE mode, Rise = Fall

- Qx (EOR 1) c> Sequencer A (Clock)
- Qx (EOF 1) c> Sequencer B (Clock)
- Quadrax (OUT 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: AHR release-detect trigger
patchIdeas.patch: EOF goes high the moment a note's release ends — perfect for chaining a second event.
steps: Set Quadrax CH 1 to AHR mode (MODE button red).
Set Rise for attack, Fall for release.
Patch your gate source → Quadrax CH 1 TRIG.
Patch Quadrax OUT 1 → VCA CV (main voice).
Patch Qx EOF 1 → a second envelope / sample trigger (fires after the note fully dies).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Qx EOF 1 — 0–5V gate at the moment release completes.
patchIdeas.hear: Every time the main note finishes fading out, a second event fires — echo-tail, reverb swell, ghost note, or a sampled 'breath' after the word. EOR instead fires at the peak, if you want the second event on the way in.
patchIdeas.diagram:
QX + QUADRAX:
* CH 1: AHR mode

- Gate Source (Gate) g> Quadrax (CH 1 TRIG)
- Quadrax (OUT 1) >> VCA (CV)
- Qx (EOF 1) t> Tail Voice (Trig) // fires when release ends
